task: I need to send a short follow-up to a prospect who clicked an email but didn’t book a call. Can you help me draft something that sounds professional, gets to the point fast, and gives them a clear next step without sounding pushy?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, professional follow-up you can use:

**Subject:** Quick follow-up

Hi [Name],  

Thanks for taking a look at my email. I wanted to follow up in case this is still on your radar. If it makes sense, you can book a quick call here: [link]. If not, no worries at all — just let me know.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **warmer/more personal version**
- a **more direct sales version**
- or **3 subject line options**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, close. But it’s a little too soft / generic for what I need.

I’d want it tighter and a bit more specific about the next step, like:

Hi [Name],

Just following up on my last email — wanted to see if a quick call makes sense. If so, you can grab a time here: [link]. If not, no problem.

Best,
[Your Name]

If you can, give me 2-3 versions: one super short, one a little warmer, and one more sales-y.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 3 tighter versions in that style:

### 1) Super short
Hi [Name],  

Just following up on my last email — if a quick call makes sense, you can grab a time here: [link]. If not, no problem.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

### 2) A little warmer
Hi [Name],  

Following up on my last email — if it’s worth a quick conversation, feel free to book a time here: [link]. If now isn’t the right time, no worries.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

### 3) More sales-y
Hi [Name],  

Wanted to follow up on my last email and see if a quick call makes sense. If you’d like to explore this further, you can book a time here: [link]. If not, just let me know and I’ll close the loop.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make these sound:
- more **executive/professional**
- more **friendly/casual**
- or more **direct/strong CTA**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is better. The shorter one works, and the sales-y one is decent too. I’d probably use the middle one if I want it to feel a little less blunt.

No extra stuff needed.
